ddls:
CREATE TABLE "apis" (
  "api_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API */,
  "api_name" TEXT /* Name of the API */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the API's functionality */,
  "auth_method" TEXT /* Authentication method required for API access (e.g., apiKey) */,
  "https_enabled" INTEGER /* Indicates if HTTPS is required for the API */,
  "cors_policy" TEXT /* CORS policy for the API (e.g., Unknown, Yes, No) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the API was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the API was last updated */,
  "endpoint_url" TEXT /* Base URL of the API endpoint */,
  "api_key_param" TEXT /* Parameter name for API key authentication */,
  "api_key_description" TEXT /* Description of the API key parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("api_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "api_key" TEXT /* API key assigned to the user for authentication */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was last updated */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the request */,
  "api_id" INTEGER /* ID of the API being accessed */,
  "request_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the request was made */,
  "response_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the response was received */,
  "status_code" INTEGER /* HTTP status code of the response */,
  "response_body" TEXT /* Body of the response received from the API */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address involved in the request */,
  "domain" TEXT /* Domain involved in the request */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL involved in the request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_requests_api_id FOREIGN KEY ("api_id") REFERENCES apis ("api_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user this setting belongs to */,
  "api_id" INTEGER /* ID of the API these settings apply to */,
  "https_required" INTEGER /* Indicates if HTTPS is required for this user's requests */,
  "cors_enabled" INTEGER /* Indicates if CORS is enabled for this user's requests */,
  "timeout" INTEGER /* Timeout in seconds for API requests */,
  "retry_count" INTEGER /* Number of retries for failed API requests */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_settings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_settings_api_id FOREIGN KEY ("api_id") REFERENCES apis ("api_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "api_id" INTEGER /* ID of the API providing the response */,
  "response_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the response was received */,
  "status_code" INTEGER /* HTTP status code of the response */,
  "data" TEXT /* Serialized data returned from the API */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_responses_api_id FOREIGN KEY ("api_id") REFERENCES apis ("api_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_logs" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error log */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the API request that encountered the error */,
  "error_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the error occurred */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Description of the error encountered */,
  "error_code" TEXT /* Code identifying the type of error */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_logs_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES api_requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API version */,
  "api_id" INTEGER /* ID of the API this version belongs to */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the API */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of this version */,
  "deprecation_date" TEXT /* Date when this version is planned for deprecation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_versions_api_id FOREIGN KEY ("api_id") REFERENCES apis ("api_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "api_id" INTEGER /* ID of the API whose status is being logged */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the API (e.g., operational, down) */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the status was last checked */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_status_api_id FOREIGN KEY ("api_id") REFERENCES apis ("api_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "api_id" INTEGER /* ID of the API being analyzed */,
  "request_count" INTEGER /* Total number of requests made to the API */,
  "success_count" INTEGER /* Number of successful requests made to the API */,
  "error_count" INTEGER /* Number of failed requests made to the API */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_analytics_api_id FOREIGN KEY ("api_id") REFERENCES apis ("api_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the API category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the API category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_categories_map" (
  "map_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping entry */,
  "api_id" INTEGER /* ID of the API being mapped */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category the API belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("map_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_categories_map_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES api_categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_categories_map_api_id FOREIGN KEY ("api_id") REFERENCES apis ("api_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_activity" (
  "activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each activity log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user whose activity is being logged */,
  "activity_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the activity occurred */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity (e.g., login, logout, api_request) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_activity_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
